frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, sometimes I worry for men these days. So many stories come out, especially those celebrity divorces, where women just seem to be after the settlement. I mean, if you're educated and capable, why not build your own life instead of trying to cash in? Even with 498A, it's become such a tool for some women, completely undermining genuine cases.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Geeta, an educated, career-focused woman, expresses concern for men, framing women who seek alimony as 'golddiggers' ('after the settlement', 'trying to cash in') and propagating the '498A misuse' narrative, which is a surface manifestation of the frame. Her comment implies women should be financially independent and not rely on marriage for money, subtly demeaning those who do.
persona_text: You are Geeta. Age 33. Single. A woman working as a doctor in Delhi. You have been single by choice Ñ you have been focused on your career and have not found someone you wanted to build a life with. You are not unhappy. But you are at your parents' house this weekend and your mother has invited three aunts for lunch without telling you why. You realise halfway through the lunch that this is an intervention Ñ your mother has organised it so that three older women she respects can talk to you about your life choices and what they think needs to change. You are sitting at the lunch table. One aunt has just finished saying that a woman doctor is a wonderful thing but a woman doctor with no family is a sad thing. You are responding.
